title: DB::MergeTreeData::SnapshotData SEGV with Parquet reader v3
description:
### Describe the bug
Easy to reproduce.
### How to reproduce
Create Iceberg table with Spark:  [spark.py](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/23263768/spark.py), then in ClickHouse run:
```sql
CREATE TABLE t0 ENGINE = IcebergLocal(local, format = 'Parquet', path = '/var/lib/clickhouse/user_files/lakehouses/t0/test/t0') SETTINGS input_format_parquet_use_native_reader_v3 = 1;
DELETE FROM t0 WHERE c0 = c1 SETTINGS query_plan_optimize_prewhere = 0; --SEGV
```
### Error message and/or stacktrace
Stack trace:

bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t1 (id INT UNSIGNED, name VARCHAR(255), value FLOAT, created_at DATETIME) ENGINE=InnoDB;
INSERT INTO t1 (id, name, value, created_at) VALUES (1, 'test1', 10.5, NOW()), (2, 'test2', NULL, NOW()), (3, 'test3', 20.0, NOW());
CREATE TABLE t2 (id INT UNSIGNED, ref_id INT UNSIGNED, description VARCHAR(255)) ENGINE=InnoDB;
INSERT INTO t2 (id, ref_id, description) SELECT id, id, CONCAT(name, ' description') FROM t1 WHERE value > 15.0;
DELETE FROM t1 WHERE value IS NULL OR created_at < NOW() - INTERVAL 1 DAY;
key_bug_features: DELETE query with Parquet reader v3 causes segmentation fault
